*   _must_ provide public documentation of [substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
     to the technical report since the previous [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsWD)
    ,
*   _should_ provide public documentation of significant [editorial changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#editorial-change)
     to the technical report since the previous step,
*   _should_ report which, if any, of the Working Group’s requirements for this document have changed since the previous step,
*   _should_ report any changes in dependencies with other groups,

Possible next steps for any Working Draft:

*   Revised [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-wd)
    
*   [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-cr)
    
*   [Discontinued Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#abandon-draft)
    

#### 6.3.7. Transitioning to Candidate Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-cr)

To publish a [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
, in addition to meeting the [requirements for advancement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-reqs)
 a [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
:

*   _must_ show that the specification has met all [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
     requirements, or explain why the requirements have changed or been deferred,
*   _must_ document changes to dependencies during the development of the specification,
*   _must_ document how [adequate implementation experience](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#adequate-implementation)
     will be demonstrated,
*   _must_ specify the deadline for comments, delineating the Candidate Recommendation review period, which _must_ be **at least** 28 days after publication, and _should_ be longer for complex documents,
*   _must_ show that the specification has received [wide review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#dfn-wide-review)
    , and
*   _may_ identify features in the document as at risk. These features _may_ be removed before advancement to [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
     without a requirement to publish a new [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
    .

The first Candidate Recommendation publication after verification of having met the requirements for a [Transition Request](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#trans-req)
 is always a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
. The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ announce the publication of the [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
 to other W3C groups and to the public.

Possible next steps after a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
:

*   Return to [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-wd)
    
*   A revised [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-crrs)
    
*   A revised [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-crud)
    
*   [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-pr)
    
*   [Discontinued Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#abandon-draft)
    

[Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee)
 representatives _may_ initiate an [Advisory Committee Appeal](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#ACAppeal)
 of the decision to advance the technical report.

#### 6.3.8. Revising a Candidate Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-cr)

##### 6.3.8.1. Publishing a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-crrs)

If there are any [substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
 made to a [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
 since the previous [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
 other than to remove features explicitly identified as [at risk](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#at-risk)
, the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _must_ meet the requirements of an [update request](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#update-requests)
 in order to republish.

In addition the Working Group:

*   _must_ specify the deadline for further comments, which _must_ be **at least** 28 days after publication, and _should_ be longer for complex documents,
*   _may_ identify features in the document as [at risk](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#at-risk)
    . These features _may_ be removed before advancement to [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
     without a requirement to publish a new [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
    .

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ announce the publication of a revised [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
 to other W3C groups and to the public.

To provide timely updates and patent protection, a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
 _should_ be published within 24 months of the Working Group accepting any proposal for a substantive change (and preferably sooner). To make scheduling reviews easier, a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
 _should not_ be published more often than approximately once every 6 months.

Note: [Substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
 trigger a new Exclusion Opportunity per “Exclusion From W3C RF Licensing Requirements” in the W3C Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
.

##### 6.3.8.2. Publishing a [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-draft)
[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-crud)

A [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _should_ [publish](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
 an [Update Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-draft)
 to the W3C Technical Reports page when there have been significant changes to the previous published document that would benefit from review beyond the Working Group.

To publish a revision of a [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-draft)
, a Working Group:

*   _must_ record the group’s decision to request publication,
*   _must_ provide public documentation of [substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
     to the technical report since the previous [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
    ,
*   _should_ provide public documentation of significant [editorial changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#editorial-change)
     to the technical report since the previous [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
    ,
*   _should_ document outstanding issues, and parts of the document on which the Working Group does not have consensus,
*   _should_ report which, if any, of the Working Group’s requirements for this document have changed since the previous step,
*   _should_ report any changes in dependencies with other groups.

Note: A Working Group _does not_ need to meet the requirements of a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
 [update request](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#update-requests)
 in order to publish a [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-draft)
.

Possible next steps after a [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-draft)
:

*   Return to [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-wd)
    
*   A revised [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-crrs)
    
*   A revised [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-crud)
    
*   [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-pr)
    , if there are no [substantive change](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
     other than dropping [at risk](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#at-risk)
     features
*   [Discontinued Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#abandon-draft)
    

#### 6.3.9. Transitioning to Proposed Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-pr)

In addition to meeting the [requirements for advancement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-reqs)
,

*   The status information _must_ specify the deadline for [Advisory Committee review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
    , which _must_ be **at least** 28 days after the publication of the [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
     and _should_ be at least 10 days after the end of the last Exclusion Opportunity per ”Exclusion From W3C RF Licensing Requirements” in the W3C Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
    .

A Working Group:

*   _must_ show [adequate implementation experience](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#adequate-implementation)
     except where an exception is approved by a [Team Decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team-decision)
    ,
*   _must_ show that the document has received [wide review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#dfn-wide-review)
    ,
*   _must_ show that all issues raised during the [Candidate Recommendation review period](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-review-period)
     have been [formally addressed](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#formally-addressed)
    ,
*   _must_ identify any substantive issues raised since the close of the [Candidate Recommendation review period](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-review-period)
    ,
*   _must not_ have made any [substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
     to the document since the most recent [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
    , other than dropping features identified [at risk](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#at-risk)
    .
*   _may_ have removed features identified in the [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
     document as [at risk](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#at-risk)
     without republishing the specification as a [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
    .

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
:

*   _must_ announce the publication of a [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
     to the [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#AC)
    , and _must_ begin an [Advisory Committee Review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
     on the question of whether the specification is appropriate to [publish](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
     as a [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    .
*   _may_ approve a [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
     with minimal [implementation experience](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#implementation-experience)
     where there is a compelling reason to do so. In such a case, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
     _must_ explain the reasons for that decision, and that information _must_ be included in the [Call for Review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#reviewform)
     proposing advancement to [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    .

Since a [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 _must not_ include any [substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
 from the [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
 it is based on, to make any [substantive change](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
 to a [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
 the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _must_ return the specification to [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
 or [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsWD)
.

A [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
 may identify itself as intending to allow new features ([class 4 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
) after its initial publication as a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, as described in [§ 6.3.11.4 Revising a Recommendation: New Features](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-features)
. Such an allowance cannot be added to a [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 previously published as a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 that did not allow such changes.

Possible Next Steps:

*   Return to [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-wd)
    
*   Return to [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-cr)
    
*   [Recommendation status](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-rec)
    , (the expected next step).
*   [Discontinued Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#abandon-draft)
    

[Advisory Committee representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
 _may_ initiate an [Advisory Committee Appeal](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-appeal)
 of the decision to advance the technical report.

#### 6.3.10. Transitioning to W3C Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-rec)

The decision to advance a document to [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 is a [W3C Decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-w3c-decision)
.

In addition to meeting the [requirements for advancement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-reqs)
,

*   A [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
     _must_ identify where errata are tracked, and
*   A [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
     _must not_ include any [substantive changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
     from the [Proposed Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsPR)
     on which it is based.
*   If there was any [dissent](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-Dissent)
     in Advisory Committee reviews, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
     _must_ publish the substantive content of the dissent to W3C and the general public, and _must_ [formally address](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#formally-addressed)
     the comment at least 14 days before publication as a [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    .
*   [Advisory Committee representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
     _may_ initiate an [Advisory Committee Appeal](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-appeal)
     of the [W3C decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-w3c-decision)
    
*   The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
     _must_ announce the publication of a [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
     to [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#AC)
    , other W3C groups and to the public.

Possible next steps: A [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 normally retains its status indefinitely. However it _may_ be:

*   republished as a [revised Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-rec)
    , or
*   republished as a [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-cr)
     to be developed towards a revised [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    , or
*   declared [superseded or obsolete](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rec-rescind)
    , or
*   [rescinded](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rec-rescind)
    .

#### 6.3.11. Revising a W3C Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-rec)

This section details the process for making changes to a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
.

##### 6.3.11.1. Revising a Recommendation: Markup Changes[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-markup)

A [Working group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _may_ request republication of a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 to make corrections that do not result in any changes to the text of the specification. (See [class 1 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
.)

If there is no [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 chartered to maintain a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _may_ republish the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 with such changes incorporated.

##### 6.3.11.2. Revising a Recommendation: Editorial Changes[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-editorial)

[Editorial changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#editorial-change)
 to a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 require no technical review of the intended changes. A [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
, provided there are no votes against the [decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#group-decision)
 to publish, _may_ request publication of a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 to make this class of change without passing through earlier maturity stages. (See [class 2 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
.)

If there is no [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 chartered to maintain a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _may_ republish the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 with such changes incorporated, including [errata](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#erratum)
 and [Team corrections](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team-correction)
.

##### 6.3.11.3. Revising a Recommendation: Substantive Changes[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-substantive)

A [candidate correction](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-correction)
 can be made normative and be folded into the main text of the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, once it has satisfied all the same criteria as the rest of the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, including review by the community to ensure the technical and editorial soundness of the [candidate amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
. To validate this, the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 must request a [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
, followed by an [update request](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#update-requests)
. See [§ 6.3.11.5 Incorporating Candidate Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#change-review)
.

Alternatively, a [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _may_ incorporate the changes and [publish as a Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-wd)
—or, if the relevant criteria are fulfilled, [publish as a Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#transition-cr)
—and advance the specification from that state. (See [class 3 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
.)

Note: If there is no [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 chartered to maintain a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 cannot make substantive changes and republish the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
. It can, however, informatively highlight problems and desirable changes using [errata](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#erratum)
 and [candidate corrections](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-correction)
 and republish as described in [the previous section](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-editorial)
.

##### 6.3.11.4. Revising a Recommendation: New Features[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-features)

New features (see [class 4 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
) may be incorporated into a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 explicitly identified as [allowing new features](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#allow-new-features)
 using [candidate additions](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-addition)
. A [candidate addition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-addition)
 can be made normative and be folded into the main text of the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 using the same process as for [candidate amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
, as detailed in [§ 6.3.11.3 Revising a Recommendation: Substantive Changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revised-rec-substantive)
.

Note: This prohibition against new features unless explicitly allowed enables third parties to depend on Recommendations having a stable feature-set, as they have prior to the 2020 revision of this Process.

To make changes which introduce a new feature to a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 that does not [allow new features](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#allow-new-features)
, W3C _must_ create a new [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
, following the full process of [advancing a technical report to Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rec-advance)
 beginning with a new [First Public Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#fpwd)
.

##### 6.3.11.5. Incorporating Candidate Amendments[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#change-review)

A Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments verifies acceptance by the W3C community of [candidate amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
 by combining an [AC Review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
 with a patent exclusion opportunity.

The [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
 must be announced to other W3C groups, the public, and the [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee)
. The announcement _must_:

*   Identify whether this is a Last Call for Review of Proposed Corrections, Last Call for Review of Proposed Additions, or Last Call for Review of Proposed Corrections and Additions.
*   Identify the specific [candidate amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
     under review as proposed amendments (proposed corrections/proposed additions).
*   Specify the deadline for review comments, which must not be any sooner than 60 days from the Call for Review.
*   Solicit review and, if it does not already have it, implementation experience.

The combination of the existing [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 with the [proposed amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 included in the [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
 is considered a [Patent Review Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#patent-review-drafts)
 for the purposes of the Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
. Also, the review initiated by the [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
 is an [Advisory Committee Review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
.

Note: [Last Call for Review of Proposed Additions](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-for-review-of-proposed-additions)
 and [Last Call for Review of Proposed Corrections and Additions](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-for-review-of-proposed-corrections-and-additions)
 can only be issued for [Recommendations](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 that [allow new features](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#allow-new-features)
.

A [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _may_ batch multiple [proposed amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 into a single [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
. To facilitate review, a [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
 on a given specification _should not_ be issued more frequently than approximately once every 6 months.

At the end of the [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
, the [W3C Decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-w3c-decision)
 may either be to reject the [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
, or to clear the [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 for advancement as is, or to return the proposal to the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 with a request to [formally address](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#formally-addressed)
 comments made on the changes under review. If the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 needs to amend a [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 in response to review feedback it must issue another [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
 on the revised change before it can be incorporated into the main text.

Once all comments on a [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 have been [formally addressed](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#formally-addressed)
, and after the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 can show [adequate implementation experience](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#adequate-implementation)
 and the fulfillment of all other requirements of Recommendation text, it may incorporate the [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 into the normative [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 by issuing an [update request](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#update-requests)
 for publication of the updated [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
.

To ensure adequate review of [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 combinations, only [proposed amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 included in the most recent [Last Call for Review of Proposed Amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-review)
 can be incorporated into the normative [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 text. (Thus if incorporation of a [proposed amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 is postponed, it may need to be included in multiple Last Calls for Review of Proposed Amendments.)

#### 6.3.12. Regression on the Recommendation Track[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rec-track-regression)

A [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 may republish a [Recommendation-track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-recommendation-track)
 [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 at a lower [maturity stage](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#maturity-stages)
 by fulfilling the requirements to transition to that maturity stage, as described above.

Additionally, with the approval of the [TAG](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-architecture-group)
 and the [AB](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-board)
 the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _may_ return the [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 to a lower [maturity stage](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#maturity-stages)
 in response to [wide review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#dfn-wide-review)
 or a [formal objection](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#FormalObjection)
.

#### 6.3.13. Retiring Recommendation Track Documents[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#tr-end)

Work on a technical report _may_ cease at any time. Work _should_ cease if W3C or a [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 determines that it cannot productively carry the work any further.

##### 6.3.13.1. Abandoning an Unfinished Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#abandon-draft)

Any [Recommendation-track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-recommendation-track)
 [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 no longer intended to advance or to be maintained, and that is not being rescinded, _should_ be [published](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
 as a [Discontinued Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#discontinuedREC)
, with no [substantive change](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#substantive-change)
 compared to the previous publication. This can happen if the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 decided to abandon work on the report, or as the result of an [AC Review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
 requiring the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 to discontinue work on the technical report before completion. If a [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 is made to [close](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GeneralTermination)
, W3C _must_ re-[publish](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
 any unfinished [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 on the Recommendation track as [Discontinued Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#discontinuedREC)
.

Such a document should include in its status section an explanation of why it was discontinued.

A [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _may_ resume work on such a [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 within the scope of its charter at any time, by re-[publishing](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
 it as a [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsWD)
.

##### 6.3.13.2. Rescinding a Candidate Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rescind-cr)

The process for rescinding a [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
 is the same as for rescinding a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
.

##### 6.3.13.3. Abandoning a W3C Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rec-rescind)

It is possible that W3C decides that implementing a particular [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 is no longer recommended. There are three designations for such specifications, chosen depending on the advice W3C wishes to give about further use of the specification.

W3C _may_ obsolete a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, for example if the W3C Community decides that the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 no longer represents best practices, or is not adopted and is not apparently likely to be adopted. An [Obsolete Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsObs)
 _may_ be restored to normal [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, for example because despite marking it Obsolete the specification is later more broadly adopted.

W3C _may_ declare a Recommendation Superseded if a newer version exists which W3C recommends for new adoption. The process for declaring a Recommendation Superseded is the same as for declaring it Obsolete, below; only the name and explanation change.

W3C _may_ rescind a Recommendation if W3C believes there is no reasonable prospect of it being restored for example due to burdensome patent claims that affect implementers and cannot be resolved; see the W3C Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
 and in particular “W3C Royalty-Free (RF) Licensing Requirements” and “PAG Conclusion”.

W3C only rescinds, supersedes, or obsoletes entire Recommendations. A [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 can be both superseded and obsolete. To rescind, supersede, or obsolete some part of a Recommendation, W3C follows the process for [modifying a Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-rec)
.

Note: For the purposes of the W3C Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
 an [Obsolete](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsObs)
 or [Superseded Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsSup)
 has the status of an active [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
, although it is not recommended for future implementation; a [Rescinded Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RescindedRec)
 ceases to be in effect and no new licenses are granted under the Patent Policy.

Supersede, Obsolete or Rescind a W3C Recommendation Recommendation (Rec) REC A major problem and an AC review can lead to a Recommendation being Rescinded. There are no new IPR licences issued under the W3C Patent Policy, and reinstating the Recommendation requires going through the full Rec-track process again. Major problem, AC review Rescinded Recommendation - no new IPR licenses Rescinded No new IPR licenses No reinstatement path With little uptake, following AC review a specification may become an Obsolete Recommendation Minimal usage, AC review Obsolete Recommendation Obsolete If there is new uptake, with AC review an Obsolete Recommendation may return to normal Recommendation status New uptake, AC review Replaced by a new version, AC review Superseded Recommendation Superseded A Superseded Recommendation can become a normal Recommendation with AC review Oops! The Web still needs this, AC review

##### 6.3.13.4. Process for Rescinding, Obsoleting, Superseding, Restoring a Recommendation[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#deactivation)

The process of rescinding, obsoleting, superseding, or restoring a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 can be initiated either by a request from the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 or via a request from any of the following:

*   The [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
     who produced, or is chartered to maintain, the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    
*   The [TAG](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-architecture-group)
    , if there is no such [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
    
*   Any individual who made a request to the relevant [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
     as described above, or the [TAG](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-architecture-group)
     if such a group does not exist, to obsolete, rescind, supersede, or restore a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    , where the request was not answered within 90 days
*   5% of the members of the Advisory Committee

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ then submit the request to the [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#AC)
 for review. For any [Advisory Committee review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
 of a proposal to rescind, obsolete, supersede, or restore a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_:

*   announce the proposal to all [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
     [Chairs](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GeneralChairs)
    , and to the Public, as well as to the [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee)
    
*   indicate that this is a proposal to Rescind, Obsolete, Supersede, or restore, a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
     as appropriate
*   identify the [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
     by URL
*   publish a rationale for the proposal
*   identify known dependencies and solicit review from all dependent [Working Groups](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
    
*   solicit public review
*   specify the deadline for review comments, which _must_ be at least 28 days after the announcement

and _should_

*   identify known implementations.

If there was any [dissent](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-Dissent)
 in the [Advisory Committee review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ publish the substantive content of the dissent to W3C **and the public**, and _must_ [formally address](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#formally-addressed)
 the dissent at least 14 days before publication as an [Obsolete](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsObs)
 or [Rescinded Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RescindedRec)
.

The [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#AC)
 _may_ initiate an [Advisory Committee Appeal](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-appeal)
 of the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
's decision.

W3C _must_ publish an [Obsolete](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsObs)
 or [Rescinded Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RescindedRec)
 with up to date status. The updated version _may_ remove the main body of the document. The Status of this Document section _should_ link to the explanation of [Obsoleting and Rescinding W3C Specifications](https://www.w3.org/2016/11/obsoleting-rescinding/)
 [\[OBS-RESC\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-obs-resc "Obsoleting and Rescinding W3C Specifications")
 as appropriate.

Once W3C has published a Rescinded Recommendation, future W3C technical reports _must not_ include normative references to that technical report.

Note: W3C strives to ensure that all Technical Reports will continue to be available at their version-specific URL.

### 6.4. The Note Track (Notes and Statements)[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#note-track)

#### 6.4.1. Group Notes[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Note)

A Group Note (NOTE) is published to provide a stable reference for a useful document that is not intended to be a formal standard.

[Working Groups](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
, [Interest Groups](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsIG)
, the [TAG](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-architecture-group)
 and the [AB](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-board)
 _may_ publish work as [Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
. Examples include:

*   supporting documentation for a specification, such as explanations of design principles or use cases and requirements
*   non-normative guides to good practices

Some [Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 are developed through successive Draft Notes before publication as a full [Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
, while others are [published](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
 directly as a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
. There are few formal requirements to [publish](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing)
 a document as a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 or [Draft Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-note)
, and they have no standing as a recommendation of W3C but are simply documents preserved for historical reference.

Note: The W3C Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
 does not apply any licensing requirements or commitments for [Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 or [Draft Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-note)
.

#### 6.4.2. Publishing Notes[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#publishing-notes)

In order to publish a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 or [Draft Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-note)
, the group:

*   _must_ record their decision to request publication as a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
     or [Draft Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-note)
    , and
*   _should_ publish documentation of significant changes to the technical report since any previous publication.

Both [Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 and [Draft Notes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-note)
 can be updated by republishing as a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 or [Draft Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-note)
. A [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 _may_ remain a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 indefinitely.

If a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 produced by a [chartered group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#chartered-group)
 is no longer in scope for any group, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _may_ republish the [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 with [class 1](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
 changes incorporated, as well as with [errata](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#erratum)
 and [Team corrections](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team-correction)
 annotated.

#### 6.4.3. Elevating Group Notes to W3C Statement status[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-statement)

A W3C Statement is a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 that has been endorsed by W3C as a whole. In order to elevate a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 to [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 status, A group _must_:

*   show that the document has received [wide review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#dfn-wide-review)
    .
*   record the group’s decision to request publication as a [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
    .
*   show that all issues raised against the document since its first publication as a [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
     have been [formally addressed](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#formally-addressed)
    .
*   provide public documentation of any [Formal Objections](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#FormalObjection)
    .

A [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 specifying implementable technology _should not_ be elevated to [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 status; if it does, the request to publish as a [Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 _must_ include rationale for why it should be elevated, and why it is not on the [Recommendation track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-recommendation-track)
.

Once these conditions are fulfilled, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ then begin an Advisory Committee Review on the question of whether the document is appropriate to publish as a [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
. During this review period, the [Note](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGNote)
 _must not_ be updated.

The decision to advance a document to [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 is a [W3C Decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-w3c-decision)
. [Advisory Committee representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
 may initiate an [Advisory Committee Appeal](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-appeal)
 of the decision.

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 must announce the publication of a [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 to the [Advisory Committee](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee)
, other W3C groups, and the public.

#### 6.4.4. Revising W3C Statements[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#revising-memo)

Given a recorded [group decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#group-decision)
 to do so, groups can request publication of a [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 with [editorial changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#editorial-change)
—including [candidate amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
—without any additional process.

A [candidate amendment](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
 can be folded into the main text of the [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
, once it has satisfied all the same criteria as the rest of the [Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
, including review by the community to ensure the substantive and editorial soundness of the [candidate amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
. To validate this, the group must request an [Advisory Committee review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
 of the changes it wishes to incorporate. The specific [candidate amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
 under review _must_ be identified as [proposed amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 just as in a [Last Call for Review of Proposed Corrections](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#last-call-for-review-of-proposed-corrections)
.

The decision to incorporate [proposed amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 into [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
 is a [W3C Decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#def-w3c-decision)
. [Advisory Committee representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
 may initiate an [Advisory Committee Appeal](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-appeal)
 of the decision.

### 6.5. The Registry Track[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registries)

A registry documents a data set consisting of one or more associated registry tables, each table representing an updatable collection of logically independent, consistently-structured registry entries. A registry has three associated components:

*   the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
    , defining how the [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
     are structured and maintained
*   one or more [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
    , holding the data set represented by the [registry](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
     (the registry data)
*   one or more referencing specifications, which make use of the [registry](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
    

The purposes of maintaining a [registry](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
 can include:

non-collision

Avoiding the problem of two entities using the same value with different semantics.

non-duplication

Avoiding the problem of having two or more different values in use with the same semantics.

information

Providing a central index where anyone can find out what a value means and what its formal definition is (and where it is).

submission

Ease of adding new terms, including by stakeholders external to the [custodian](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#custodian)
 organization.

consensus

Promoting a clear consensus of the community on the terms.

This section of the W3C Process provides a specialized process facilitating the publication and maintenance of such [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
, particularly those required by or closely related to [W3C Recommendations](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
.

Note: Not every table in a specification is a potential registry. If the intent or effect is that the table enumerates all the possibilities the authors of the specification expect or envisage, then the table by itself is enough. Similarly, if the table is managed by the Working Group and only updated as part of specification update, then the complexities of registry management are not needed.

#### 6.5.1. Registry Definitions[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#reg-def)

A registry definition defines what each [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
 is and how it is maintained. It _must_:

*   Define the scope and purpose of each [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
    .
*   Define the fields of each [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
     and their constraints (e.g. values must be drawn from a defined set, or be unique, or only reference publicly available resources, etc.)
*   Define the policy for changes to existing entries, such as
    *   whether entries can be deleted or deprecated
    *   whether entries can be changed after being published, and what kinds of changes are allowed
    *   whether previously-deleted unique identifiers can be re-used, or are reserved indefinitely
*   Define the method and criteria by which changes are proposed, approved, and incorporated. (For example, a [registry](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
     could define that changes to [registry entries](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-entry)
     can be proposed using a particular web form or email address, that they must be accompanied by certain background information, or that they do or do not need to be approved by any member of a particular Working Group.)
*   Identify the custodian of the [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
    : the entity to which requests for [registry changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-change)
     must be sent, and which is responsible for evaluating whether such requests satisfy the criteria defined in the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
    .
    
    The [custodian](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#custodian)
     may be the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
    , the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
    , or a delegated entity. The [custodian](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#custodian)
     for all [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
     in a single [registry](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
     _should_ generally be the same entity.
    

#### 6.5.2. Publishing Registries[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#reg-pub)

[Registries](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
 can be published either as a stand-alone [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 on the [Registry Track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-track)
 called a registry report, or incorporated as part of a [Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
 as a registry section.

A [registry report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-report)
 or [registry section](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-section)
 is purely documentational, is not subject to the W3C Patent Policy, and _must not_ contain any requirements on implementations. For the purposes of the Patent Policy [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
, any [registry section](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-section)
 in a [Recommendation track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-recommendation-track)
 document is not a normative portion of that specification.

The [registry report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-report)
 or [registry section](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-section)
 _must_:

*   Clearly label the [registry report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-report)
    /[section](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-section)
    , its [tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
    , and its [registry definitions](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
     as such, including a link to [§ 6.5 The Registry Track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registries)
     in this Process.
*   Include the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
     for each of its [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
    .
*   Provide the [registry data](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-data)
     by either:
    *   Including the entire contents of each [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
        , either inline in the report (e.g. formatted as a table, or list, or other appropriate representation), or in a machine-readable file published as part of the [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
        , or (preferably) both.
    *   Linking to one or more standalone [Registry Data Reports](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-data-report)
         containing the [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
         in human-readable form, machine-readable form, or (preferably) both.
*   Include, if the [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
     is provided in a machine-readable file, a definition of the format of that file.

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ make available a means for interested parties to be notified of any updates to a [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
.

Note: Since the Process does not impose requirements on changes to the contents of a [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
 other than those imposed by the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
, acceptance of proposed [registry changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-change)
 on behalf of the [custodian](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#custodian)
 and publication of an updated [registry report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-report)
 that contains only [registry changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-change)
 since the previous publication can be automated if satisfaction of those rules can be automatically verified.

Rules for publication and advancement on the Registry Track are identical to that of the [Recommendation Track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-recommendation-track)
 with the following exceptions:

*   [Registry reports](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-report)
     are not subject to the [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
    , and therefore none of their publications correspond, to [First Public Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#fpwd)
    , [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsWD)
    , or [Patent Review Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#patent-review-drafts)
     for the purposes of the [\[PATENT-POLICY\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-patent-policy "The W3C Patent Policy")
    .
*   For the same reason, there is no equivalent to [Rescinded Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RescindedRec)
     nor to [Rescinded Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#rescinded-candidate-recommendation)
     for [Registries](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
    .
*   The equivalent of [Working Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsWD)
     is called Draft Registry[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#draft-registry)
    .
*   The equivalent of [Candidate Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsCR)
     is called Candidate Registry[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-registry)
    , with [Candidate Recommendation Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-snapshot)
     and [Candidate Recommendation Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-recommendation-draft)
     corresponding to Candidate Registry Snapshot and Candidate Registry Draft[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-registry-draft)
    .
*   The equivalent of [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
     is called W3C Registry; [Obsolete Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsObs)
     and [Superseded Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsSup)
     correspond to Obsolete Registry[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#obsolete-registry)
     and Superseded Registry[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#superseded-registry)
    .
*   There is no equivalent to the Proposed Recommendation phase. Instead, an [Advisory Committee Review](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
     is started upon publication of each [Candidate Registry Snapshot](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-registry-snapshot)
    .
*   Changes that add new features (i.e. [class 4 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
    ) are allowed in all [W3C Registries](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-registry)
    , without needing the to explicitly indicate that this is allowed.

#### 6.5.3. Updating Registry Tables[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#reg-table-update)

Changes to the contents of a [registry table](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
 that are in accordance with the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
, (i.e. [Class 5 changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#correction-classes)
) can be made by re-publishing the [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 that contains the affected table, without needing to satisfy any other requirements for the publication (not even Working Group consensus, unless this is required by the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
). Such [registry changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-change)
 do not trigger new [Advisory Committee Reviews](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-review)
, nor Exclusion Opportunities, and do not require verification via an [update request](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#update-requests)
, even for [technical reports](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 at maturities where this would normally be expected. Such publications can be made even in the absence of a [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 chartered to maintain the registry when the [custodian](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#custodian)
 is another entity.

Note: The custodian is only empowered to make [registry changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-change)
. If the Working Group establishing the registry wishes to empower the custodian to add commentary on individual entries, this needs to be part of the registry table’s definition. If other changes are desired, they must be requested of the responsible Working Group—or in the absence of a Working Group, of the Team.

Changes to the [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
 made in accordance with [candidate](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#candidate-amendment)
 or [proposed amendments](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#proposed-amendments)
 to the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
 which would not be allowed by the unamended [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
 _must_ be identified as such.

#### 6.5.4. Registry Data Reports[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#reg-data-reports)

When the [registry data](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-data)
 is published in a separate [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 from its [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
, that [report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 is called a Registry Data Report. This [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
:

*   _Must_ link to the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
     establishing the [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
     that it contain.
*   _May_ contain informative introductory text, examples, and notes about the [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
     and [entries](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-entry)
     that it contains. (Changes to these parts are deemed to be [editorial changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#editorial-change)
    ).

[Registry Data Reports](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-data-report)
 do not have maturity stages in and of themselves; The maturity stage of the [registry](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry)
 whose [data](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-data)
 they record is that of the [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 holding the [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
.

Anytime a change is made to a [registry definition](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-definition)
, the Working Group _must_ update and republish any document holding the corresponding [registry tables](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-table)
 to make it consistent with these changes.

Given a recorded [group decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#group-decision)
 to do so, the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 _may_ republish the [Registry Data Report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-data-report)
 to incorporate [editorial changes](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#editorial-change)
. If there is no [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 chartered to maintain this registry, the [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _may_ do so instead.

#### 6.5.5. Specifications that Reference Registries[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#reg-ref-specifications)

Registries document values, they do not define any architectural or interoperability requirements related to those values. All architectural and interoperability requirements pertaining to [registry entries](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#registry-entry)
 _must_ be contained in the specifications that reference the registry, and are therefore subject to the processes (including approval and intellectual property provisions) applicable to those referencing specifications.

If there are entries that must be implemented, or any other such restrictions, they _must_ be defined or documented in the referencing specification without dependency on the registry.

[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#example-cbb39e74)
For example, “All implementations must implement the `Basic-Method` as defined in the registry” is not acceptable; a change to the definition of the `Basic-Method` in the registry would then affect conformance. Instead, the requirement must be complete in the specification, directly or by reference to another specification. For example “All implementations must recognize the name `Basic-Method`, and implement it as defined by section yy of IETF RFC xxxx”. (The Registry should nonetheless contain Basic-Method as an entry.)

### 6.6. Switching Tracks[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#switching-tracks)

Given a [Group decision](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#group-decision)
 to do so, [Working Groups](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
 can republish a [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 on a different track than the one it is on, under the following restrictions:

*   A [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
     that is or was a [W3C Recommendation](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#RecsW3C)
    , [W3C Statement](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#statement)
    , or [Patent Review Draft](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#patent-review-drafts)
     cannot switch tracks.
*   A [technical report](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
     should not switch away from the [Recommendation Track](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#w3c-recommendation-track)
     without due consideration of the Patent Policy implications and approval of W3C’s legal counsel if the [Working Group](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GroupsWG)
     envisions a likelihood of returning to it later.

[Technical reports](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#technical-report)
 that switch tracks start at their new track’s initial maturity stage, while retaining any established identity (url, shortname, etc.).

### 6.7. Further reading[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#further-reading)

Refer to ["How to Organize a Recommendation Track Transition"](https://www.w3.org/Guide/transitions)
 [\[TRANSITION\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-transition "Organize a Technical Report Transition")
 in the [Art of Consensus](https://www.w3.org/Guide/)
 [\[GUIDE\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-guide "The Art of Consensus, a guidebook for W3C Working Group Chairs and other collaborators")
 for practical information about preparing for the reviews and announcements of the various steps, and [tips on getting to Recommendation faster](https://www.w3.org/2002/05/rec-tips)
 [\[REC-TIPS\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-rec-tips "Tips for Getting to Recommendation Faster")
. Please see also the [Requirements for modification of W3C Technical Reports](https://www.w3.org/2003/01/republishing/)
 [\[REPUBLISHING\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-republishing "In-place modification of W3C Technical Reports")
.

7\. Dissemination Policies[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#dissemination)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### 7.1. Public Communication[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#pub-com)

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 is responsible for managing communication within W3C and with the general public (e.g., news services, press releases, managing the website and access privileges, and managing calendars). Members _should_ solicit review by the Team prior to issuing press releases about their work within W3C.

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 makes every effort to ensure the persistence and availability of the following public information:

*   [W3C technical reports](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Reports)
     whose publication has been approved. Per [W3C IPR Policies](https://www.w3.org/Consortium/Legal/2002/ipr-notice-20021231)
    , W3C technical reports (and software) are available free of charge to the general public.
*   A [mission statement](https://www.w3.org/Consortium/mission)
     [\[MISSION\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-mission "The W3C Mission statement")
     that explains the purpose and mission of W3C, the key benefits for Members, and the organizational structure of W3C.
*   Legal documents, including the [Membership Agreement](https://www.w3.org/Consortium/Agreement/Member-Agreement)
     [\[MEMBER-AGREEMENT\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-member-agreement "W3C Membership Agreement")
     and documentation of any legal commitments W3C has with other entities.
*   The Process Document.
*   Public results of W3C activities and [Workshops](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#GAEvents)
    .

To keep the Members abreast of W3C meetings, [Workshops](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#EventsW)
, and review deadlines, the Team provides them with a regular (e.g., weekly) news service and maintains a [calendar](https://www.w3.org/participate/eventscal)
 [\[CALENDAR\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-calendar "Calendar of all scheduled official W3C events")
 of official W3C events. Members are encouraged to send schedule and event information to the Team for inclusion on this calendar.

### 7.2. Confidentiality Levels[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#confidentiality-levels)

There are three principal levels of access to W3C information (on the W3C website, in W3C meetings, etc.): public, Member-only, and Team-only.

While much information made available by W3C is public, “Member-only” information is available to authorized parties only, including representatives of Member organizations, [Invited Experts](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#invited-expert-wg)
, the Advisory Board, the TAG, and the Team. For example, the [charter](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#WGCharter)
 of some Working Groups _may_ specify a [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 confidentiality level for group proceedings.

“Team-only” information is available to the Team and other authorized parties.

Those authorized to access [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 and [Team-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Team-only)
 information:

*   _must_ treat the information as confidential within W3C,
*   _must_ use reasonable efforts to maintain the proper level of confidentiality, and
*   _must not_ release this information to the general public or press.

The [Team](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#team)
 _must_ provide mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 information and ensure that authorized parties have proper access to this information. Documents _should_ clearly indicate whether they require [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 confidentiality. Individuals uncertain of the confidentiality level of a piece of information _should_ contact the Team.

[Advisory Committee representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
 _may_ authorize [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 access to [Member representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#member-rep)
 and other individuals employed by the Member who are considered appropriate recipients. For instance, it is the responsibility of the [Advisory Committee representative](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
 and other employees and official representatives of the organization to ensure that Member-only news announcements are distributed for internal use only within their organization. Information about Member mailing lists is available in the [New Member Orientation](https://www.w3.org/Member/Intro)
 [\[INTRO\]](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#biblio-intro "Process, Patent Policy, Finances, Specs management, Strategic vision (Member-only access)")
.

### 7.3. Changing Confidentiality Level[](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#confidentiality-change)

As a benefit of membership, W3C provides some [Team-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Team-only)
 and [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 channels for certain types of communication. For example, [Advisory Committee representatives](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#advisory-committee-representative)
 can send [reviews](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#ACReview)
 to a [Team-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Team-only)
 channel. However, for W3C processes with a significant public component, such as the technical report development process, it is also important for information that affects decision-making to be publicly available. The Team _may_ need to communicate [Team-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Team-only)
 information to a Working Group or the public. Similarly, a Working Group whose proceedings are [Member-only](https://www.w3.org/policies/process/20231103/#Member-only)
 _must_ make public information pertinent to the technical report development process.
